title: Fixing Missing Right Child Node Handling in a Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Given a binary tree root node, implement a function to count the number of leaf nodes in the tree. A leaf node is a node with no children.
statement.inputFormat: A binary tree represented as a list of [val,left, right] where left and right are child nodes
statement.outputFormat: An integer representing the number of leaf nodes in the tree
statement.notes: You can use a recursive approach or a stack for the traversal.

examples.explanation: There are 3 leaf nodes with values 9, 15, and 7.

hints.text: Use a recursive or stack-based approach to traverse the tree.

editorial.title: Missing Right Child Node Handling
editorial.intuition: You need to consider the right child of each node during traversal.
editorial.bruteForce: This approach can lead to incorrect results if you miss the right child node.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a recursive or stack-based approach to ensure you visit all nodes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: By considering all nodes, you ensure accurate counting.
editorial.dryRun: Start with a sample input and manually traverse the tree to understand the bug.
commonMistakes: Missing right child nodes during traversal
